sentence: Acemoglu and Restrepo present a framework for understanding the effects of automation and other types of technological changes on labor demand, and use it to interpret changes in US employment over the recent past.
label: keep
reason: First statement: framework for automation effects on labor demand.

sentence: At the center of their framework is the allocation of tasks to capital and labor—the task content of production.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: task allocation to capital and labor (task content of production).

sentence: Within this framework, automation enables capital to replace labor in tasks it was previously engaged in.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: automation lets capital replace labor in tasks.

sentence: This process shifts the task content of production against labor because of a displacement effect.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: this shifts task content against labor = displacement effect.

sentence: However, the effects of automation are counterbalanced by the creation of new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: counterbalanced by creation of new tasks (comparative advantage).

sentence: The introduction of new tasks changes the task content of production in favor of labor because of a reinstatement effect.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: new tasks shift content toward labor = reinstatement effect.

sentence: This reinstatement effect always raises the labor share and labor demand.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: reinstatement always raises labor share and labor demand.

sentence: In contrast to the displacement effect, which shifts the task content of production against labor, the reinstatement effect changes the task content of production in favor of labor.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: contrast of displacement vs reinstatement direction.

sentence: Whereas the displacement effect reflects capital replacing labor in existing tasks, the reinstatement effect arises from the creation of new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: displacement = replace in existing tasks vs reinstatement = new tasks.

sentence: The authors provide an empirical decomposition to assess these dynamics in practice.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: authors provide an empirical decomposition.

sentence: Their empirical decomposition suggests that the slower growth of employment over the last three decades is accounted for by an acceleration in the displacement effect.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: slower employment growth = acceleration in displacement effect.

sentence: This acceleration in the displacement effect has been especially pronounced in manufacturing.
label: keep
reason: Distinct new entity: acceleration pronounced in manufacturing.

sentence: By comparison, a weaker reinstatement effect has also contributed to the observed employment trends.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: weaker reinstatement effect contributed to trends.

sentence: Although the reinstatement effect always raises the labor share and labor demand, the empirical evidence indicates that a weaker reinstatement effect has contributed to the slower growth of employment over this period.
label: keep
reason: Distinct framing tying empirical evidence to weaker reinstatement; new qualifier.

sentence: Their decomposition further identifies slower growth of productivity than in previous decades as an additional factor accounting for the slower growth of employment.
label: keep
reason: Distinct new factor: slower productivity growth accounts for slower employment growth.

sentence: In his analysis of labor-market dynamics, Autor advances the thesis that automation does indeed substitute for labor, as it is typically intended to do.
label: keep
reason: First statement: Autor thesis that automation substitutes for labor.

sentence: However, he emphasizes that automation also complements labor, raises output in ways that lead to higher demand for labor, and interacts with adjustments in labor supply.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: automation also complements labor, raises output and demand.

sentence: By comparison, the complementary channel operates through productivity gains that expand output and, in turn, raise the demand for labor.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: complementary channel via productivity gains expanding output.

sentence: Despite the substitutive intent behind automation, the interdependence among inputs ensures that gains in one domain propagate value to others.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: input interdependence propagates value across domains.

sentence: Automation's interaction with adjustments in labor supply further underscores that the net employment consequence is not determined by substitution alone.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: interaction with labor-supply adjustments; net effect not substitution alone.

sentence: Taken together, the thesis and complementarity argument depict automation as simultaneously displacing and augmenting labor within the same work process.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: synthesis - automation simultaneously displaces and augments labor.

sentence: Identification strategy: variation in exposure to robots—defined from industry-level advances in robotics and local industry employment.
label: keep
reason: First statement: identification strategy (exposure to robots) cites [4].

sentence: Population: US labor markets.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: population = US labor markets.

sentence: Effect estimate with uncertainty: One more robot per thousand workers reduces the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 percentage points and wages by 0.42%.
label: keep
reason: First statement of robot-density effect: 0.2 pp + 0.42% wages, cites [4,5,6,7].

sentence: Outcome: employment and wages.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: outcome = employment and wages.

sentence: Method: a novel methodology to estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations, using a Gaussian process classifier.
label: keep
reason: First statement of method: 702 occupations, Gaussian process classifier, cites [8,9,10].

sentence: Exposure measure: probability of computerisation.
label: keep
reason: First statement of exposure measure = probability of computerisation, cites [8,9,10].

sentence: By leveraging this geographic and industrial variation in robot exposure, the analysis links broad technological advances in robotics to measurable changes in local labor-market conditions.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: geographic/industrial variation links robotics advances to local conditions.

sentence: Within these US labor markets, the study quantifies the relationship between increasing robot density and the outcomes of employment and wages.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: study quantifies robot-density vs employment/wage relationship.

sentence: At this defined dose level, one more robot per thousand workers reduces the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 percentage points.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates robot-density employment effect (0.2 pp), SAME cites [4,5,6,7], no new number (drops wage).

sentence: Whereas the employment outcome is expressed in percentage points, the wage outcome is expressed as a percentage change, reflecting the distinct metrics applied to each labor-market endpoint.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim: pp-vs-percentage metric contrast between employment and wage outcomes.

sentence: Despite the reliance on a single dose parameter—one more robot per thousand workers—the estimated effects on both employment and wages are consistently negative in direction.
label: keep
reason: Distinct claim AND different cite set [4,6,7]: effects consistently negative in direction.

sentence: In sum, the study documents that variation in exposure to robots—defined from industry-level advances in robotics and local industry employment—produces quantifiable negative effects on both employment and wages within US labor markets.
label: keep
reason: Summative distinct claim cites [4]: negative effects on employment AND wages.

sentence: The study introduced a novel methodology to estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations, using a Gaussian process classifier.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates method (702/Gaussian), SAME cites [8,9,10], no new number/entity.

sentence: At its core, the exposure measure was defined as the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure = probability of computerisation, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The authors developed a novel methodology to estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations, using a Gaussian process classifier.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates method (702/Gaussian) verbatim variant, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The exposure measure chosen for the analysis was the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: Although the methodological apparatus centered on a Gaussian process classifier, the construct being estimated remained the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure (Gaussian classifier vs probability of computerisation), SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The investigation employed a novel methodology to estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations, using a Gaussian process classifier.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates method (702/Gaussian), SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The probability of computerisation was the exposure measure adopted.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The analytical design rested on a novel methodology to estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations, using a Gaussian process classifier.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates method (702/Gaussian), SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The exposure measure operationalized in this framework was the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: Although the classifier was Gaussian process-based, the estimated quantity was the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure, SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: The exposure measure was the probability of computerisation.
label: paraphrase_repeat
reason: Restates exposure measure (the bare canonical form), SAME cites [8,9,10].

sentence: New tools like ControlNet ( 6), image-to-image ( 7), and depth-to-image ( 8) offer users increasingly granular control over the text-to-image pipeline, transforming what was once a labor-intensive process involving human ingenuity and manual skill into a mechanical process guided by human ideas and sense-making ( 9, 10).
label: keep
reason: First ControlNet sentence, citation set {156} (distinct set from the glued variant).

sentence: The release of Midjourney in February 2022 shows no immediate effect, but following Stable Diffusion 1.5’s release in October 2022, the AI-assisted creators’ idea frontier undergoes accelerated expansion (i.e., the raw number of divergent artifacts increases) relative to that of the nonadopters.
label: keep
reason: First statement: Midjourney/Stable-Diffusion-1.5 idea-frontier expansion, set {156,157,158}.

sentence: In particular, text-to-image generative algorithms based on the latent diffusion model ( 5), such as Stable Diffusion, Midjourney, DALL-E, Imagen, and Flux, have gained traction in creative fields for creating high-fidelity digital artworks based on artists’ ideation.
label: keep
reason: First statement: latent-diffusion text-to-image algorithms (SD/Midjourney/DALL-E/Imagen/Flux), set {156,157,158}.
